title: Scrap Wood Catch-All Tray
summary: A freehand-shaped catch-all tray glued up from scrap boards and cut entirely with a jigsaw.
description:
This tray doesn't start from a template or a set of measurements — it starts from whatever scrap boards you glue up, and the shape (oval, a simple animal silhouette, a monogram) gets traced on afterward and cut freehand with a jigsaw. No CNC or router required.

Keep every board **no thicker than 3/4".** A thicker glue-up gets difficult for a jigsaw to follow accurately around a tight curve, especially through a full 3/4" of hardwood.

Because the shape is entirely up to you, this is one of the more forgiving small projects to improvise on — a wobble in the outline reads as handmade character rather than a mistake.
timeLabel: 45 min–1.5 hrs plus glue drying time

tools.note: A scroll saw or band saw also work.

steps.title: Glue up the panel
steps.body: Glue up enough scrap boards, none thicker than 3/4in, to cover your desired tray footprint. Clamp until fully cured.

steps.title: Trace your shape
steps.body: Once the glue-up is dry, trace your chosen shape onto the panel — an oval, an animal silhouette, a monogram, or whatever suits the gift.

steps.title: Cut the outline
steps.body: Cut the outline with a jigsaw, following the traced line closely.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand the edges and faces smooth, then finish as desired.
